pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 9-10) which creates the directory, and export_result.sh (line 7) which specifies DOWNLOAD_DIR variable

Verified in setup_eramba.sh which seeds two risks: 'Phishing Attacks on Employees' (line 149, contains 'Phishing') and 'Ransomware Attack on Corporate Network' (line 158, contains 'Ransomware'). export_result.sh lines 66-68 validates these keywords with case-sensitive substring matching

Metadata claim defined in task.json line 19, but NOT referenced in verifier.py and NOT validated in export_result.sh. File size is captured in export_result.sh (line 24) but never checked against minimum threshold. No validation logic enforces this constraint.

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on the Eramba GRC platform, a real open-source governance, risk, and compliance software used by thousands globally. All data is 100% synthetic, created during environment setup via SQL INSERT statements seeding exactly 3 risks: 'Phishing Attacks on Employees', 'Ransomware Attack on Corporate Network', and 'Insider Threat - Unauthorized Data Exfiltration'. The task verifies export of risk data containing the keywords 'Phishing' and 'Ransomware', with target download directory /home/ga/Downloads. The metadata claim for minimum file size (100 bytes) is defined but not actually enforced in the verification logic.
